message:
🐛 Fix stack overflow on Arcade page and improve chunk error detection (#1701)

The Arcade page (and all dashboard pages) had `cards` in the dependency
array of `handleConfigureCard`, causing all card callbacks to be
recreated on every card state change. With 21 game cards on the Arcade
page, this triggered cascading re-renders that could exhaust the call
stack (27 "Maximum call stack size exceeded" errors in GA4).

Fix: Change `openConfigureCard` in useDashboardModals to use an
internal ref for the cards array instead of accepting it as a parameter.
This makes the callback stable — it never changes regardless of card
state updates. The ref is wired automatically in useDashboard.

Also add MIME type mismatch detection to chunk error handling (catches
"is not a valid JavaScript MIME type" errors when the server returns
HTML instead of JS for missing chunks after deployment).

Files changed:
- dashboardHooks.ts: openConfigureCard uses ref instead of parameter
- Arcade.tsx, DashboardPage.tsx, Deploy.tsx, Clusters.tsx,
  DashboardRuntime.tsx: Remove cards from handleConfigureCard deps
- chunkErrors.ts: Add MIME type error detection

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
